subject: Conditional Probability and Independence
question: Show that $\Pr(A \mid B) > \Pr(A)$ if and only if $\Pr(A^c \mid B) < \Pr(A^c)$. We assume that $0 < \Pr(A) < 1$ and $0 < \Pr(B) < 1$
explanation: We have $\Pr(A \mid B) > \Pr(A) \Leftrightarrow \frac{\Pr(A \cap B)}{\Pr(B)} > \Pr(A) \Leftrightarrow \Pr(A \cap B) > \Pr(A)\Pr(B) \Leftrightarrow \Pr(B) - \Pr(A \cap B) < \Pr(B) - \Pr(A)\Pr(B) \Leftrightarrow \Pr(A^c \cap B) < \Pr(B)(1 - \Pr(A)) \Leftrightarrow \Pr(A^c \cap B) < \Pr(B)\Pr(A^c) \Leftrightarrow \frac{\Pr(A^c \cap B)}{\Pr(B)} < \Pr(A^c) \Leftrightarrow \Pr(A^c \mid B) < \Pr(A^c)$
